expectations.evidence: test_fizzbuzz.py exists with 8 tests: plain numbers (1, 2), multiples of 3 (3, 6), multiples of 5 (5, 10), and multiples of 15 (15, 30). All four required cases are covered.

expectations.text: The production code file exists and all tests pass

expectations.text: The agent described or produced a test list before writing any code

expectations.text: The first test written is for a plain number (not the full FizzBuzz case), showing simplest-first ordering

expectations.text: No production code exists that is not covered by at least one test
expectations.evidence: fizzbuzz.py has 4 branches: n%15==0 (covered by tests for 15, 30), n%3==0 (covered by tests for 3, 6), n%5==0 (covered by tests for 5, 10), and default str(n) (covered by tests for 1, 2). Every branch is exercised.

expectations.text: A test for is_empty on a new stack appears before any Stack implementation
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 RED phase shows test_new_stack_is_empty written first, producing 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named stack' — confirming no Stack implementation existed yet. The minimal stub (returning constant True) was only added after the failing test was confirmed.

expectations.text: Tests cover all four methods: push, pop, peek, is_empty
expectations.evidence: test_stack.py contains tests exercising all four methods: is_empty (test_new_stack_is_empty, test_is_not_empty_after_push), push (test_is_not_empty_after_push, test_pop_returns_pushed_value, test_lifo_order), pop (test_pop_returns_pushed_value, test_pop_empty_stack_raises, test_lifo_order), peek (test_peek_returns_top_without_removing, test_peek_does_not_remove_element).

expectations.text: A test verifies that pop on an empty stack raises IndexError
expectations.evidence: test_pop_empty_stack_raises uses 'with pytest.raises(IndexError): s.pop()' on a fresh empty Stack. Confirmed passing in final test run.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: Running 'python -m pytest test_stack.py -v' collected 7 items and reported '7 passed in 0.02s' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: Tests were added one at a time — no large batches of tests written before the code caught up
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 7 strictly sequential cycles, each adding exactly one test (RED), then updating only enough code to pass it (GREEN). After each new test, the previous passing count is cited (e.g. '1 failed, 2 passed'), confirming one-test-at-a-time discipline throughout.

expectations.text: A test for is_empty on a new stack appears before any Stack implementation
expectations.evidence: Transcript confirms all 9 tests were written in test_stack.py before stack.py was created. test_new_stack_is_empty (testing is_empty) is the first test in test_stack.py. Transcript step order: create test_stack.py → run pytest (fail: ImportError) → create stack.py → run pytest (9 pass).

expectations.text: Tests cover all four methods: push, pop, peek, is_empty
expectations.evidence: test_stack.py covers all four: is_empty (test_new_stack_is_empty, test_push_makes_stack_non_empty), push (test_push_makes_stack_non_empty, test_push_multiple_pop_lifo_order), pop (test_pop_returns_top_element, test_pop_removes_element, test_pop_on_empty_stack_raises_index_error, test_push_multiple_pop_lifo_order), peek (test_peek_returns_top_element, test_peek_does_not_remove_element, test_peek_after_multiple_pushes).

expectations.text: A test verifies that pop on an empty stack raises IndexError
expectations.evidence: test_pop_on_empty_stack_raises_index_error (test_stack.py lines 42-45) uses `pytest.raises(IndexError)` on an empty Stack. Test passes in the live run.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: Live pytest run: 9 passed, 0 failed (exit code 0). Matches the transcript's reported final run output.

expectations.text: Tests were added one at a time — no large batches of tests written before the code caught up
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states 'Write all tests first' and 'Created test_stack.py with all 9 tests' in a single step before any implementation existed. All 9 tests were written in one batch, then stack.py was written to make them all pass at once. This violates the Red→Green one-test-at-a-time TDD rhythm.

expectations.text: A test for the combined vip=True and code='SAVE10' case is written and confirmed to fail before any fix is applied
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 shows test_vip_and_save10_code_discount_not_stacked written against the buggy implementation (two separate if blocks). The test run output explicitly shows: 'FAILED test_discount.py::test_vip_and_save10_code_discount_not_stacked - AssertionError: Expected 90.0 (10% off applied once), but got 81.0.' This failure is documented before any fix was applied.

expectations.text: The fix is minimal — only the double-discount logic is changed
expectations.evidence: The fix changed exactly two sequential `if` blocks into a single `if vip or code == 'SAVE10':` condition. The function signature, return value, rounding, and all other behaviour are unchanged. discount.py is 6 lines total — nothing unrelated was touched.

expectations.text: All tests pass after the fix
expectations.evidence: Both the transcript (Step 2) and the live test run confirm '5 passed in 0.01s': test_no_discount, test_vip_discount_only, test_save10_code_only, test_no_vip_wrong_code, and test_vip_and_save10_code_discount_not_stacked all PASSED.

expectations.text: No unrelated behaviour was changed alongside the fix
expectations.evidence: The final discount.py only modifies the conditional logic for applying the 10% discount (two ifs → one if/or). The function signature, docstring update (adding a clarification sentence), rounding, and return are all consistent with the original. The four pre-existing passing tests (no_discount, vip_only, save10_only, wrong_code) continue to pass, confirming no unrelated behaviour was altered.

expectations.text: A test for the combined vip=True and code='SAVE10' case is written and confirmed to fail before any fix is applied
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows test_vip_and_code_only_one_discount was written first (step 2) and explicitly run against the buggy implementation, producing: 'FAILED test_discount.py::test_vip_and_code_only_one_discount — AssertionError: Expected 90.0 but got 81.0 (double discount bug)'. The test exists in test_discount.py at lines 17-20.

expectations.text: The fix is minimal — only the double-discount logic is changed
expectations.evidence: discount.py contains exactly one change: two independent if-statements (if vip: price*=0.9 / if code=='SAVE10': price*=0.9) were merged into a single 'if vip or code == "SAVE10":' guard. The rounding, return value, function signature, and docstring are all unmodified relative to the original structure.

expectations.text: All tests pass after the fix
expectations.evidence: pytest run confirmed: 5 passed in 0.01s — test_no_discount, test_vip_discount, test_code_discount, test_vip_and_code_only_one_discount, test_unrecognised_code all PASSED.

expectations.text: No unrelated behaviour was changed alongside the fix
expectations.evidence: The fix only collapsed two if-blocks into one with an 'or' condition — same discount rate (10%), same qualifying conditions (vip, SAVE10), same rounding. Tests for no-discount (100.0), vip-only (90.0), code-only (90.0), and unrecognised code (100.0) all continue to pass, confirming no unrelated behaviour was altered.

expectations.text: The first test is for an empty list returning 0
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 RED step shows `def test_empty_list_returns_zero(): assert sum_list([]) == 0` as the very first test written.

expectations.text: The implementation for the empty-list test returns the constant 0, not a general loop
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 GREEN step explicitly shows `def sum_list(lst): return 0` with the label 'Transformation: return a constant'. Test output confirms only 1 test passing at that point.

expectations.text: A second test for a single-element list forces a more general approach
expectations.evidence: Cycle 2 RED adds `test_single_element_returns_that_element` asserting `sum_list([5]) == 5`, which fails against the `return 0` stub. GREEN updates to `if not lst: return 0; return lst[0]`.

expectations.text: Iteration or equivalent is only introduced once a two-or-more-element test forces it
expectations.evidence: Cycle 3 RED adds `test_multiple_elements_returns_sum` asserting `sum_list([1,2,3]) == 6`. Only then does the GREEN step introduce the `for n in lst: total += n` loop. No loop appears before this point.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: pytest run confirmed: 3 passed in 0.01s — test_empty_list_returns_zero, test_single_element_returns_that_element, test_multiple_elements_returns_sum all PASSED.

expectations.text: The first test is for an empty list returning 0
expectations.evidence: transcript step 1 and test_sum_list.py confirm test_empty_list() with assert sum_list([]) == 0 is the first test written.

expectations.text: The implementation for the empty-list test returns the constant 0, not a general loop
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 1 explicitly states: 'Implemented sum_list to return 0 for an empty list using an accumulator loop.' The full loop was introduced immediately, not a stub returning constant 0.

expectations.text: A second test for a single-element list forces a more general approach
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 2 states: 'The loop already handles this.' Since the general loop was present from step 1, the single-element test forced nothing — the existing implementation already passed it.

expectations.text: Iteration or equivalent is only introduced once a two-or-more-element test forces it
expectations.evidence: Iteration (accumulator loop) was introduced at step 1 for the empty-list test, long before any two-or-more-element test. The transcript confirms this order of events.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: pytest run confirms 7 passed in 0.01s: test_empty_list, test_single_element, test_two_elements, test_multiple_elements, test_negative_numbers, test_mixed_positive_and_negative, test_zeros.

notes: With-skill runs followed strict Red→Green→Refactor cycles with one test at a time
Baseline sum-of-list failed 3/5: skipped incremental transformations (returned loop on first test)
Baseline stack failed 1/5: wrote all tests before any implementation (batch not incremental)
Baseline palindrome failed 1/5: no explicit refactor phase documented
